seoTitle: How to Receive & Pay for Inventory in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to receive and pay for inventory in QuickBooks Online, from purchase orders to payment, so stock and costs stay accurately tracked.
intro: Getting inventory into your books correctly means tracking it from the purchase order through to the actual payment. This guide walks through receiving inventory and recording the payment in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Create or open the purchase order
steps.body: Start from an existing purchase order for the inventory items you're receiving.

steps.heading: Receive the inventory
steps.body: Mark the items as received, updating your stock quantities to reflect what's now on hand.

steps.heading: Convert to a bill
steps.body: Turn the received inventory into a bill if payment isn't happening immediately.

steps.heading: Record the payment
steps.body: Pay the bill once funds go out, linking the payment back to the original purchase order and bill.

faq.question: Do I need a purchase order to receive inventory?
faq.answer: It's not strictly required, but starting from a purchase order keeps quantities and costs consistent through the process.

faq.question: What if I only receive part of an order?
faq.answer: You can mark a partial quantity as received, leaving the rest open on the purchase order until the remainder arrives.

faq.question: Does receiving inventory update my stock counts automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, marking items as received updates your on-hand inventory quantities in QuickBooks Online.

faq.question: Can I pay for inventory before receiving it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can record a payment or deposit ahead of receiving goods, though it's typically tracked as a prepayment until the items arrive.
